content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: March 15, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Ted Prim

Subject: Procedure for Reviewing Opinions Issued by Agencies Interpreting Their Conflicts of Interest Codes

At its meeting of March 7, 1978; the Fair Political Practices Commission approved the staff's internal procedure for reviewing opinions issued by agencies interpreting their Conflict of Interest Codes. The Commission indicated that it wished to review only those opinions which involved a complex or difficult interpretation of the Code or the Act. The Commission also made it clear that such agency opinions neither confer immunity of the type described in Section 83114(a) nor bind the Commission with respect to future interpretations of the Code or the Act.

TP:mfa

embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting the Commission's March 7, 1978 approval of a staff procedure for reviewing agency opinions interpreting Conflict of Interest Codes, clarifying that the Commission will review only complex interpretations and that such opinions do not confer immunity or bind future FPPC interpretations.
